volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Braggart Soldier (Section 4.2.1015b - Section 4.2.1064b)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Miles Gloriosus, or The Braggart Captain

sections.label: Section 4.2.1015b
sections.original: Infidós celas : ego súm tibi firme fidus .
sections.translation: From the uninitiated you conceal them . I am sure and trustworthy to you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1016
sections.original: Cedo sígnum , si harunc Báccharum es .
sections.translation: Give me the sign , if you are one of these votaries [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1016b
sections.translation: A certain lady loves a certain gentleman .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1017
sections.translation: Faith , many ladies do that indeed .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1017b
sections.original: At nón multae de dígito donum míttunt .
sections.translation: But not many ladies send a present from off their fingers .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1018
sections.original: Enim cógnovi nunc , fécisti modo mi éx proclivo plánum .
sections.translation: Aye , I know now . You ’ ve now made the matter level for me instead of steep . But is there a certain person here ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1019b
sections.translation: Either he is or he is not .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1019c
sections.translation: Come aside with me alone , in private .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1020
sections.translation: For a short or for a lengthy conversation ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1020b
sections.translation: For three words only .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1020c
sections.translation: I ’ ll return to you this instant .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1021
sections.original: Quid ego ? híc astabo tántisper cum hac fórma et factis frústra ?
sections.translation: What ? Shall I be standing here in the meanwhile , with such charms and valorous deeds , thus to no purpose ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1022
sections.original: Patere átque asta , tibi ego hánc do operam .
sections.translation: Submit to it and wait ; for you am I doing this service .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1022b
sections.translation: Make haste ; I am tortured with waiting .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1023
sections.original: Pedetémptim ( tu hoc scis ) tráctari satiúst hasce huius modi mércis .
sections.translation: You know that commodities of this kind are only wont to be reached step by step .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1024
sections.original: Age age út tibi maxume cóncinnumst .
sections.translation: Well , well ; as is most agreable to yourself .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1024b
sections.translation: There is no stone more stupid than this fellow . I now return to you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1025
sections.original: redeo ád te . quid me vóluisti ?
sections.translation: ( To MILPHIDIPPA .) What would you with me ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1025b
sections.translation: In the way in which I received it of you a short time since , I bring you back your clever lot ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.1026b
sections.translation: my story is as though she were dying with love for him .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1026c
sections.translation: That I understand .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1027
sections.original: Conlaúdato formam ét faciem et virtútis commemoráto .
sections.translation: Do you commend his beauty and his appearance , and make mention of his prowess .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1028
sections.original: Ad eám rem habeo omnem aciém , tibi uti dudúm iam demonstrávi .
sections.translation: For that purpose I am armed at all points [*] , as I have shown you before already .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1029
sections.original: Tu cétera cura et cóntempla et de meís venator vérbis .
sections.translation: On the other hand , do you give all attention , and be on the watch , and take your cue [*] from my words .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1030
sections.original: Aliquám mihi partem hodie óperae des deníque , tandem ades , remelígo .
sections.translation: Prithee do now , in fine , give me some share in the business ; step this way this instant , I beg .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1031
sections.translation: Here I am . If you wish for aught , give me your commands .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1031b
sections.translation: What is she saying to you ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1031c
sections.translation: She is saying that her mistress is lamenting , and , in tears , is tormenting and afflicting herself because she wishes for you , and because she possesses you not ; for that reason has she been sent here to you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1034
sections.translation: Bid her approach .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1034b
sections.original: At scin quid tú facias ? facitó fastidi plénum ,
sections.translation: But do you know how you are to act ? Pretend that you are full of disdain ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.1035
sections.original: quasi nón lubeat ; me inclámato , quia síc te volgo vólgem .
sections.translation: as though it pleased you not ; exclaim against me , because I make you so common to the mob .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1036
sections.translation: I remember , and I ’ ll follow your instructions .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1036b
sections.original: Vocon érgo hanc quae te quaérit ?
sections.translation: I ’ ll call her , then , who is inquiring after you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1037
sections.translation: If she wants anything , let her come .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1037b
sections.translation: Wench , if you want anything , step this way .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1037c
sections.translation: Save you , charmer [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1038
sections.original: Meum cógnomentum cómmemoravit . di tíbi dent quaecumque óptes .
sections.translation: She makes mention of my surname . May the Gods grant you whatever you may desire .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1039
sections.translation: To pass life with you is the wish of —

sections.label: Section 4.2.1039b
sections.translation: You are wishing too much .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1039c
sections.translation: I am not speaking of myself , but of my mistress , who is dying for you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1040b
sections.translation: Many others are wishing for the same thing , who have not the opportunity .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1041b
sections.original: Écastor haud mírum , si te habes cárum ,
sections.translation: By my troth , ’ tis not to be wondered at ; you set a high value on yourself — a person so handsome , and so illustrious for his prowess , and so valorous in his deeds ! O ! was there ever any one more worthy to be a man ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1043b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , the filthy fellow is not a human being ; indeed , I think there is something more human in a vulture [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1044b
sections.translation: Now I shall make myself of importance ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.1045
sections.original: nunc quom íllaec me sic cónlaudat .
sections.translation: since she so praises me up .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1045b
sections.original: Viden tu ígnavum , ut sese ínfert ?
sections.translation: Do you see the blockhead , how he struts ? ( To PYRGOPOLINICES .) But will you not answer her ; she is the woman that ’ s come from the lady whom I was mentioning just now .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1047
sections.original: Qua ab íllarum ? nam ita me óccursant multaé : meminisse haud póssum .
sections.translation: But from which one of them ? For there are so many courting me , I cannot remember them all .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1048
sections.original: Ab illá quae digitos déspoliat suos ét tuos digitos décorat .
sections.translation: From her who strips her own fingers and adorns your fingers ; for I delivered to him ( pointing to PALAESTRIO ) that ring from her who is sighing for you , and then he to you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1050
sections.original: Quid núnc tibi vis , muliér ? memora .
sections.translation: Tell me , wench , what is it you want then ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1050b
sections.original: Vt quae té cupit , eam ne spérnas ,
sections.translation: That you will not despise her who is sighing for you ; who lives now but in your life : whether she is to exist or not , her hope is in you alone .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1052
sections.translation: What does she want then ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1052b
sections.original: Te compéllare et complécti et contrectáre .
sections.translation: To talk with you , to embrace you , and to be intimate with you . For unless you bring her succour , she will soon be quite desponding in her mind . Come , my Achilles , let that be done which I entreat ; save her , charmer , by your charming ways .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1055
sections.original: expróme benignum ex te íngenium , urbícape , occisor régum .
sections.translation: Call forth your kind disposition , stormer of cities , slayer of kings .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1056
sections.original: Eu hercle ódiosas res . quótiens hoc tibi , vérbero , ego interdíxi ,
sections.translation: O ! by my troth , ’ tis a vexatious thing ! ( To PALAESTRIO .) How often , whip-scoundrel , have I forbidden you to make promises of my attention thus common .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1057b
sections.translation: Do you hear that , hussy ? I have told you already , and I now tell you again , unless a fee is given to this boar-pig [*] , he cannot possibly throw away his attentions in any quarter .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1061
sections.translation: A fee shall be given , as large as he shall demand .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1061b
sections.original: Talentúm Philippi huic opus aúri est ;
sections.translation: He requires a talent of gold , in Philippean pieces . Less he will take from no one .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1062b
sections.translation: O , by my troth , but that ’ s too little , surely .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1063
sections.original: Non míhi avaritia umquam ínnatast : satis hábeo divitiárum ,
sections.translation: By nature there ’ s no avarice in me ; I have riches enough . I ’ faith ! I ’ ve more than a thousand measures [*] full of Philippean gold coins .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1064b
sections.translation: Besides your treasures .
